concerns.analysis: The image manifest indicates that the agency logo on page 1 ('Logo featuring large block letters "NC"...') has a 'valid' status of false due to an extraction error ('No module named \'PIL\''). Because the base64 data is corrupted, this meaningful image (which establishes the issuing agency) will not render in the browser, constituting a broken image.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: NC Water Resources ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY logo on page 1
concerns.output_quote: Image manifest reports '"valid": false' for the NC logo image (b64_len: 23820)

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (6): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 6; Page 3 of 6
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "To:"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

The source PDF features bolded and underlined text to emphasize the introductory topics in the bulleted lists on pages 3, 4, and 5 (e.g., 'Air Quality', 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Costs'). The extraction output loses all of this structural emphasis, rendering these list item prefixes as plain text without any bold or underline formatting.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Air Quality – Citizens expressed concerns about fly ash from the sites and the possible health concerns.
concerns.output_quote: Air Quality – Citizens expressed concerns about fly ash from the sites and the possible health concerns.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.
